roadStages.label: Venture Board Review
roadStages.subtitle: in 8 weeks
roadStages.tagline: Model, pressure-test, get ready to pitch

tooltips.disabledFeature: Demo flow continues elsewhere. Try the highlighted button.

presenterClickPath.action: Let's go to the Test Track

0.1.headline: Meet Miles.
0.1.subhead: An AI companion for Honda intrapreneurs.

milesMessages.text: Hey Ian, here's where your electric lawnmower project stands today. I pulled together everything that landed in your inbox, Slack, and project log this week. Before we pick a thread, what's the one thing you most want to move forward?

summaryCards.label: 3 emails
summaryCards.tooltip: Prototype feedback: runtime · swap-battery logistics · cut quality on tall/wet grass · weight & maneuverability · deck width vs. productivity · noise (positive). Follow-up, Laura (sole-op landscaper): Ran the demo on 3 lawns Tuesday. Got through ~2.5 properties before charge dropped, so she needs a full morning on one charge or a battery she can swap mid-route. So quiet her 7am client didn't complain for the first time. Fix the runtime and she's in.

1.1.promptMessage: Where do you want to start? I've got a recommendation if you want one.

inputModes.label: Voice
inputModes.tooltip: Voice and Sandbox modes aren't in this preview.

inputModes.label: Sandbox
inputModes.tooltip: Voice and Sandbox modes aren't in this preview.

1.1.footerReason: Segment choice is your highest-leverage move right now. It shapes your pricing, channels, and proof points for the next 90 days.

ianInput.text: Honestly, I need to nail down who actually buys this before I build anything else.

1.2.ianMessage: I need to validate who my customer will be.
1.2.milesResponse: From our last session I pulled three landscaper profiles. Each one points the lawnmower in a different direction. Who's jumping out at you?

personas.name: Laura
personas.pain: Gas mowers are loud, clients complain

personas.name: Matt
personas.tooltip: Demo flow continues with Laura. Try the highlighted card.

personas.name: Diana
personas.bio: Half-acre lot, mows weekly in season
personas.tooltip: Demo flow continues with Laura. Try the highlighted card.

ianInput.text: Laura's the one I keep picturing. Let's start with her.

1.3.milesIntro: Before we build, let's pin down the real problem. Five questions. Answer what you know, and flag what's still a guess. Which one are you least sure about?

questions.a: Gas mowers: loud, fumes, constant maintenance
Honda's brand will obviously win this

questions.hint: Point to evidence, not a feeling…

ianInput.text: Here's roughly who I'm picturing and where it breaks down for her.
ianInput.assetNote: REPLACE/CONFIRM PATH: Ian's hand-drawn Sharpie sketch (solo landscaper + gas mower, LOUD!!/fumes/always breaking, 2026 regs, crossed-out $?/battery?/too $$$).

sketchToForm.milesStructuringLine: Got it. Three of these came straight off your sketch. Let me line them up so we can pressure-test them.

fieldSources.note: not in the sketch; carried from earlier, stays flagged as a hunch
Ian crossed out the price/battery guesses himself; no evidence yet, stays empty

sketchToForm.milesClosingLine: The 'why you' I pulled from our earlier chat, and it's still a hunch. And you crossed out the price stuff yourself, so 'how do you know' is the real gap. Want to test it?

1.3b.milesIntro: Now the honest part. Which of these are hunches? I sorted your claims left to right. Anything on the left is still opinion. Want to defend one, or go test it?
1.3b.log: 3 hunches caught. Go talk to people before building.

ianInput.text: Yeah, the Honda-brand one is a hunch. I want to go test it, not defend it.

1.3c.milesIntro: You caught three hunches back there, all about what landscapers actually want and will pay. Before you build, you need evidence on those. Here are five ways to get it. Where do you want to start?

options.label: Customer interviews
options.ianReply: Let's start with the five interviews.
options.milesReply: That's the one. Five calls, all three hunches tested directly, fastest signal you'll get this week.

options.label: Direct observation
options.ianReply: I was going to ride along on a route first.
options.milesReply: Good instinct, and we'll do it, but a ride-along is richest once you know what to watch for. Right now you've got three open hunches about what Laura values and will pay. Five interviews answer those head-on. Do those first, ride along second.

options.label: Smoke test
options.ianReply: What about a landing page to test demand?
options.milesReply: Tempting, but a landing page before you know the value prop just gives you a noisy number. You'd be testing a guess with a guess. Come back to this after the interviews.

options.label: Competitor teardown
options.ianReply: Should I tear down Greenworks and EGO first?
options.milesReply: That tells you what already exists, not what Laura actually values. Useful later for positioning, not for the question you're stuck on now.

options.label: Existing research scan
options.ianReply: Didn't we already do the research scan?
options.milesReply: We did, that's the 3 papers and 2 surveys from last week. It didn't answer willingness-to-pay, which is exactly why we're here.

1.3d.milesIntro: Your evidence plan points to customer interviews first. Mary is a strong place to start, since she already flagged the runtime problem in your prototype test. Here's what you know going in.

personaCard.name: Mary
dayInLife: Up at 5 AM, four properties before noon
Truck, trailer, two gas mowers, blower, edger
Books new clients through Facebook and word of mouth
painPoints: First start of the morning wakes the whole neighborhood
Three clients have asked her to switch to electric
wouldPayFor: Same runtime as gas, won't accept a downgrade
whereToFind: Regional trade shows (4 in the next 90 days)

1.3d.milesFollowup: Ready to book that first call? Or want to refine the script first?

ianInput.text: Mary looks right. Book the call, but let's tighten the script first.

bullets: Booked 3 customer conversations for next week
Logged hypothesis: Sole-entrepreneur landscapers will pay a premium for quiet, electric equipment.
1.4.milesFooter: Curious what you'll hear. When you're back from those interviews, we'll head to The Garage, where you connect with colleagues who've built this kind of thing before.

1.5.milesIntro: Hello again, Ian. From your last conversation, it sounds like supply chain is where you're stuck. How do you want to keep going? You could meet someone who's solved this, or keep working through your customer personas.

bridgeCard.name: Priya Okafor
bridgeCard.title: Project Manager, Honda R&D
bridgeCard.note: Built e-bike battery supply chain · saves 6 weeks on internal safety review

options.label: Keep working on personas
options.milesReply: Personas are worth a revisit, but the supplier cost is your riskiest open question right now. Let's meet Priya first. You can come back to the personas anytime.

ianInput.text: Supply chain's the thing keeping me up. If someone's already solved this, I'd rather talk to them than guess.
ianInput.voiceNote: Shown as a sent audio clip / voice memo, the one voice moment in the flow.

2.1.milesMessage: Ian, I found three people who've been through the supplier gauntlet. Here's how they stack up, but Priya's the closest fit for what you're stuck on.

recommended.name: Priya Okafor
recommended.title: Project Manager, Honda R&D
recommended.reason: Solved your exact problem: built the e-bike battery supply chain from scratch in 2022. Closest match, and she can save you about 6 weeks on the internal safety review.

recommended.name: Daniel Reyes
recommended.title: Operations Lead, Honda Manufacturing
recommended.reason: Knows the supplier landscape broadly, but hasn't done battery sourcing specifically.
recommended.inventedNote: INVENTED placeholder name, swap in a real contact.

recommended.name: Karen Whitfield
recommended.title: Honda alum, founded & exited a hardware startup
recommended.reason: Strong on fundraising and the seed conversation, lighter on supply chain.
recommended.inventedNote: INVENTED placeholder name, swap in a real contact.

2.1.emptyNetworkMsg: You haven't connected with anyone yet.

actions.label: Browse directory
actions.tooltip: The full directory is not in this demo preview.

options.label: Connect with Priya
options.ianReply: Priya it is, she's the closest to my exact problem.
options.milesReply: Agreed. I'll reach out and find time on your calendar.

options.label: Connect with Daniel
options.milesReply: Worth knowing for when you scale, but he hasn't done battery sourcing. For the cost question you're stuck on this week, Priya's the sharper call.

options.label: Connect with Karen
options.ianReply: Should I talk to Karen first?
options.milesReply: Save Karen for when you're prepping the seed conversation, that's her strength. She's lighter on supply chain, which is the thing in your way right now.

ianInput.text: Priya's the obvious first call, but who else has been through the supplier gauntlet?

2.2.milesMessage: Good news. While you were at lunch, Priya got back to me, and I put a hold on your calendar for Thursday at 2 PM. Does that work, or should I find another time?

meetingCard.title: Project Manager, Honda R&D
meetingCard.context: She's already navigated the supplier and safety review process you're about to hit. She estimates she can save you 6 weeks.

actions.label: Suggest another time
actions.tooltip: Demo flow continues with Thursday 2 PM.

2.3.milesMessage: Good meeting with Priya. I've logged the key takeaways and added her to your network. Anything she said that you want to dig into before we move on?
takeaways: Priya's supplier contacts added to your Key Partners
Battery cost actuals revised: $103/unit (vs. your $140 assumption)
She can save you 6 weeks on the internal safety review

ianInput.text: The $103 battery actuals are huge. Update the model with her real numbers before anything else.

bullets: Connected with Priya, Honda's e-bike battery lead
3 supplier contacts from Priya's network added
Battery cost assumption corrected, $37/unit better than expected
2.4.milesFooter: Next question: do the lawnmower's numbers hold up under pressure?

cta.label: Let's go to the Test Track

ianInput.text: Feels good to not be guessing on cost anymore. What's left before the model's solid?

3.1.milesMessage: Welcome back. One thing caught my eye in the Business Model Canvas before the seed review next week. Want to take a look?

ianInput.text: Yeah, show me what's off in the revenue section.

3.2.milesMessage: Lithium pricing has moved since your February draft. Did you catch it? Worth updating your cost of materials before the seed review.
3.2.milesQuestion: Want me to plug in Priya's real numbers and update the model?

ianInput.text: Update the model with Priya's numbers.

3.3.milesIntro: Before the seed review, want to stress-test this together? Here's what a skeptical CFO is going to push on. Better you hear it from me first.

questions.header: What if Laura's willingness-to-pay is wrong?
questions.challenge: Your 12 interviews skewed toward Lauras who already complained about gas. The broader landscaper market hasn't signaled the same urgency, and your price point sits 22% above the closest competitor.
questions.response: Lead with the three paid pilots. That's revealed preference, not stated. Then acknowledge the sampling skew and show the plan to validate with a colder segment.

questions.header: Your COGS still assumes Honda's supplier pricing. What if we don't get those terms?
questions.challenge: Priya's quotes are favorable because she's been in those relationships for two years. A new venture in year one rarely gets the same pricing tier.
questions.response: Two angles. The venture is internal, so the relationships transfer, and here's the margin at standard-tier pricing. Both numbers still clear the hurdle.

questions.header: Sole-entrepreneur landscapers are 12% of the market. Where's the scale story?
questions.challenge: Even if you win 100% of Lauras, that ceiling is too low for Honda's portfolio criteria.
questions.response: Laura is the wedge, not the destination. Show the adjacency path. Once the product proves out with sole operators, the case for Matt (commercial fleet) becomes much easier to make internally and externally.

ianInput.text: Run them at me. I'd rather hear the hard ones from you than in the review.

3.4.milesMessage: Seed review is next week. Want to see exactly where you stand?
3.4.milesFollowup: 3 of 5 are solid. The two flags are normal at this stage, and there's a plan for each.

scorecard.label: Financials
scorecard.detail: updated, but margin sensitivity still tight

options.label: See my prep plan
options.milesReply: Smart. Here's what to lock before next week.

options.label: Go to the seed review
options.ianReply: I think I'm ready, let's just go to the review.
options.milesReply: You're close, but the two flags are exactly what a CFO opens with. Twenty minutes on the prep plan and you walk in covered. Worth it.

columns.label: The Garage · Month 1
bullets: 4 new contacts in the intrapreneur network

columns.label: The Test Track · Month 3
bullets: Financial model updated with current data

ianInput.text: Three months ago this was a hunch. Let's go.

T.1.subhead: Stage-by-stage. Use what you need, when you need it.

shelves.header: Spark · Ideate, test, and validate with customers
The Test Track · Model and pressure-test your work

T.1.milesCorner: Not sure where to start? Just ask, and I'll match you to the right tool.

D.1.milesMessage: Welcome back, Ian. Here's where the lawn mower project stands. What do you want to work on today?

inputModel.description: Every conversational screen shows a persistent input bar with three modes (Chat, Voice, Scratch Pad), always present like Claude's input bar. Forward motion comes from Ian appearing to respond in one of those modes, not from destination buttons. 'driver' = which mode is shown being used to advance this screen. Scratch Pad is used once (1.3), Voice once (1.5), Chat elsewhere. Three screens are branches: Ian can pick any option, Miles gives an honest tradeoff on non-ideal picks, and the screen advances only when Ian lands on the recommended pick.
